mnemonics.title: PISA - Python Inheritance Syntax Approach
mnemonics.content: P for Parent class, I for Inherit (using parentheses), S for Super() function, A for Attributes. Syntax: class ChildClass(ParentClass):

mnemonics.title: SUPER - Super Uses Parent's Essential Resources
mnemonics.content: S for super() function, U for Use to call parent methods, P for Pass self implicitly, E for Extends parent functionality, R for Returns proxy object

mnemonics.title: MRO - Method Resolution Order
mnemonics.content: Remember clockwise priority: Single→Multilevel→Hierarchical→Multiple→Hybrid. Use ClassName.mro() or __mro__ to check

mnemonics.title: DRIP - Derived Replaces Inherited Properties
mnemonics.content: D for Derived class (Child), R for Replace inherited methods, I for Inherit all parent attributes, P for Polymorphism works through overriding

acronyms.usage: Inheritance is one of the 4 pillars of OOP. Use when discussing class relationships and code reusability.
Used to describe inheritance. Dog IS-A Animal. Syntax: class Dog(Animal):
Describes composition/aggregation. Car HAS-A Engine. Not inheritance but related concept.
Defines the order in which base classes are searched when executing a method. Check using ClassName.__mro__ or ClassName.mro()
Principle that inheritance helps achieve by reusing code from parent classes in child classes.

visualTips.visualization: Picture an inverted family tree growing downward. Grandparent class at top, parent in middle, children at bottom. Arrow points from child to parent showing 'inherits from'.
Draw one straight vertical line from parent to one child. Like a single branch growing from a tree trunk.
Imagine a child with two parents standing side by side, both contributing traits. Use a 'V' shape diagram.
Think of a photocopy where the child writes over the parent's text with a different color - the child's version replaces the parent's.
Picture a relay race where super() passes the baton (control/data) from child back to parent class to continue execution.

analogies.analogy: Like a genetic inheritance in a family - children inherit eye color, height, and other traits from parents. In Python, child classes inherit attributes and methods from parent classes.
Like a Vehicle (parent) and Car (child). Vehicle defines general traits (wheels, movement), Car inherits these and adds specific ones (color, brand).
Like a family recipe - the parent has a basic recipe, but each child modifies it with their own twist while still following the base recipe structure.
Like asking your parent for permission or help before doing something. The child uses super() to access parent's methods before adding its own logic.
Like Grandfather → Father → Son lineage. Each level inherits from the previous and passes down to the next.
Like a school principal (parent) with multiple teachers (children) all inheriting the title 'Teacher' but having different subjects.
Like a child who takes traits from both mother and father. A Python class can inherit from two different parent classes.
